stem: A delivery driver is paid $15 per hour for regular hours and $34 per hour for overtime hours. Last week, the driver worked a total of 46 hours and earned $804. Overtime pay applies to any hours worked beyond 40 hours in a week. The company policy states that drivers cannot work more than 60 hours per week. How many overtime hours did the driver work last week?
explanation:
Total hours = 46 (the 60-hour maximum is extra information)
Overtime applies to hours beyond 40

Since the driver worked 46 hours total:
Regular hours = 40
Overtime hours = 46 - 40 = 6

Verification:
Earnings = 40($15) + 6($34) = $600 + $204 = $804 ✓

distractorRationale.A: Correct: Total hours 46 minus 40-hour threshold gives 6 overtime hours. Verification: 40(15) + 6(34) = 600 + 204 = 804
distractorRationale.B: Used 36 as the overtime threshold instead of 40: 46 - 36 = 10
distractorRationale.C: Thought overtime hours count as double-time: (46 - 40) × 2 = 12
distractorRationale.D: Confused the 60-hour maximum policy with the overtime threshold and subtracted total hours from the policy limit: 60 - 46 = 14
